name: Town
what: Personalized AI assistant ('Townie') that learns how you work across email, calendar, Slack, docs and messaging without setup; runs on iOS/desktop/WhatsApp/Telegram/Slack/web; raised $55M Series A (a16z + Forerunner, 2026-06-03).
why_tier: Individual-level chief-of-staff spanning many work tools proactively — explicit Tier 2 per rubric; cross-tool reach + heavy funding make it one positioning shift from team-level intelligence.
notes: Founders Jean-Denis Greze (ex-Plaid) and Tony Vincent; Series A source https://www.globenewswire.com/news-release/2026/06/03/3306172/0/en/town-raises-55m-series-a-from-a16z-and-forerunner-to-build-the-ai-assistant-that-learns-how-you-work.html ; HQ inferred from investor base.

name: Dot
what: AI data analyst delivering plain-English analytics inside Slack/Teams/email, with a 'Context Agent' that keeps metric definitions consistent across teams; 100+ customer teams incl. Duolingo, Airbyte, BlaBlaCar, Babbel.
why_tier: Tanderrum pattern — governed natural-language analytics in data vocabulary; one connector pivot from cross-tool intelligence over a company's whole stack.
notes: Stage/HQ/founded undisclosed on site; customer base skews European.

name: Querio
what: Agentic notebooks + AI agents that generate transparent SQL/Python over warehouses (Snowflake, BigQuery, Redshift, ClickHouse, +5), with a context layer for reusable logic/governance; positioned as a Looker replacement.
why_tier: Warehouse-bounded AI analyst à la Zenlytic — Astell's promise in BI vocabulary; Tier 2 data-intel.
notes: 'Querio Ltd' (UK Ltd) per site footer; stage undisclosed.

name: Carly
what: AI agent platform — CC an agent (each with its own email + memory) on a thread and it books meetings, updates CRM and runs ops across 100+/200+ work-tool integrations (Slack, Notion, Linear, Asana, HubSpot, Salesforce, +).
why_tier: Broad multi-tool agent platform with per-agent memory; automation-first but one pivot from a cross-tool intelligence layer (cf. Lindy/Coworker trajectory).

name: Pancake
what: Slack-native multi-agent platform that runs company operations via specialized sub-agents (meeting prep, inbound, SDR) with an organizational-memory system across sessions plus email/browser/phone integrations.
why_tier: Org memory + autonomous multi-tool agents acting across the company; agent-platform one pivot from Astell's lane (cf. Dust/Coworker), worth tracking despite execution-first framing.
notes: Built by Basalt (Chris Messina, Tristan Comte, François de Fitte); launched 2026; SOC II per compliance.getbasalt.ai; stage/funding undisclosed, inferred.
